title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/why-term-insurance-should-be-part-of-your-financial-planning.html
Why Should You Consider Adding Term Insurance Plan as a Part of Your Financial Planning in the Year 2025?
A term insurance plan acts as an income substitute and keeps your dependents financially protected. Including a  term insurance plan  in your financial planning can help to take care of basic needs like everyday expenses, loan repayment, children's education among a few in your absence. Term insurance plans are also tax-friendly, and thus, they can be a smart choice while planning your finances. Term insurance can be a crucial aspect of financial planning for your family.
What is Term Insurance?  A term insurance plan is a kind of life insurance. It offers protection for a set number of years, like 5, 10, 20, 30 years, etc. If the life assured passes away during this term, their family receives a fixed amount of money, called the "sum assured."
You pay a regular amount to the insurer, called a premium. In return, the insurer pays the sum assured to your nominee if you pass away during the policy term. If you outlive the policy term the policy ends without any maturity benefit . Term insurance is one of the most affordable  types of life insurance plans  as it usually offers high cover at a low cost.
Today, term insurance plans also offer add-ons like:
Critical illness rider  Accidental death benefit rider  Waiver of premium rider  Terminal illness rider   Accidental Total and Permanent Disability Benefit Rider So, it is a smart part of any  financial planning .
What is Financial Planning?  Financial planning is the process of setting goals, evaluating your current finances, and creating a structured plan to manage your income, expenses, savings, and investments. It helps you make informed decisions to achieve short-term and long-term financial goals.
In simple terms, financial planning is like a map that shows you the best path to reach your financial goals. These goals can include:
Buying a house  Paying for your child’s education  Saving for retirement  Paying off loans  Handling emergencies, etc.   Good financial planning makes sure you have enough money to meet your current needs and those in the future. It also helps reduce stress during emergencies.
A full financial plan includes many parts:
Budgeting  Saving  Buying life insurance plan  Planning taxes  Planning your retirement   Without financial planning, you may struggle to meet important goals or handle a money crisis.
Why Consider a Term Insurance Plan in Your Financial Planning?  Now that you know how to plan your finances, it’s important to understand why a term insurance plan in financial planning is useful. Here are the main reasons:
Tax Benefits  When you buy a term insurance plan, you can save on tax. Under  Section 80C  of the Income Tax Act, you can claim up to ₹1.5 lakh on the premiums you pay, if you opt for the old tax regime. Also, the money your family gets after you pass away is tax-free under Section 10(10D).
Financial Protection  Life is full of surprises, and a term insurance plan gives your family financial protection after your demise. They can use this money for daily needs, education, medical care, or any other goals.
This protection brings peace of mind.
Helps in Fulfilling Financial Obligations  You may have loans or EMIs. After you pass away, your family should not struggle to repay them. A term insurance payout can help them pay off these liabilities with ease.
For example:
Home loan:  Your spouse can pay the loan EMI comfortably.     Acts as an Income Replacement  If you are the main earner in the family, your demise can have an impact on the family financially. Term insurance functions like a financial backup for your income. Your family can get regular payments or a lump sum. They can use this to stay financially stable.
Multiple Payout Options  You can choose how your family will receive the claim amount:
Monthly income  Yearly income  Lump sum payment  A mix of both   This flexibility makes planning easier for them. It also helps in lifestyle based financial planning.
How to Choose the Right Term Insurance?  When you are planning for the future, choosing the right term insurance plan is an important part of your financial planning. Here’s a simple guide to help you pick the best term insurance plan for your needs:
Understand Your Coverage Needs  Think about your current debts, monthly expenses, and future goals like your children’s education. This will help you decide how much money ( sum assured ) your family will need after you pass away. Experts suggest choosing a sum assured that is about 10 times your yearly income. This is why keeping a  term insurance plan in your financial planning  is essential—it protects your family’s financial future.
Use a Term Insurance Calculator  You can use an online  term insurance calculator  to find out how much premium you will pay based on your age, health, coverage needs etc. This tool helps you compare different plans and pick one that fits your budget easily.
Add Extra Protection with Riders  Many term insurance plans offer extra options called riders. These include coverage for  critical illness , accidental death, waivier of premiums, terminal illness, permanent and partial disability. .
Check the Insurer’s Claim Settlement Ratio  Look for an insurance company with a high claim settlement ratio. This means they pay out most of the claims they receive, so you can trust they will support your family when needed.
In short, term insurance in financial planning is about protecting your loved ones by choosing the right coverage, using tools to find affordable premiums, adding extra benefits like riders , and selecting a reliable insurer. This is exactly why you should keep a term insurance plan in your financial planning for a secure future.
Conclusion  Preparing for tomorrow means considering both dreams and safety. A term insurance plan is more than just a "backup plan." It is a robust support to help to safeguard your family's future and lifestyle after your untimely death. In 2025, amidst shifting money habits and the growing expense of living, considering "term insurance" in a financial plan can be a smart choice.
FAQs  Why is insurance important in financial planning?  Insurance protects you and your family from unexpected financial risks, ensuring stability and peace of mind. It covers expenses from emergencies, debts, and future needs, making it a vital part of any financial plan.
What affects term insurance premium rates?  Term insurance premiums may rise due to factors like age, health etc. However, buying early usually locks in lower premiums for the entire term, so younger, healthier buyers pay less.
Why is a term insurance plan important?  A term insurance plan provides financial security by paying the sum assured to your nominee if you pass away during the  policy term , helping cover daily expenses, future goals etc without burdening them.
Why is it important to plan your financial future?  Financial planning helps you set and achieve goals like buying a home or saving for retirement, managing emergencies, avoiding debt, and maintaining financial discipline, ensuring long-term security and peace of mind.
What is the role of long-term insurance in financial planning?  Long-term insurance offers extended protection, supporting your family’s financial needs over many years, including education, loans, and living costs, making it a key part of a secure financial plan.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
